observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require:

The CAD to:

Properly document and adjust in the books the derecognition of three bank accounts declared as closed by the PNB; and

Regularly prepare and submit the BRS to COA within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. 

The CTD to:

Record in the cashbook the two bank accounts maintained with the LBP with a combined book balance of P1,087,657.71; and

Recognize in the cashbook the time deposit maintained with the LBP amounting to P1,547,535.39.

The CAD and CTD to conduct regular reconciliation of their respective records pursuant to Section 74, Chapter 3 of PD No. 1445 and Section 181.c of GAAM, Volume I, to ensure the accuracy, existence and validity of the reported balance of the CIB accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Management seeks COA’s guidance and approval to close the PNB CIB account.

Prepare the BRS for all Cash in Bank accounts.
observations.reason:
Guidance and approval of COA is requested for the closure of the PNB CIB account.

Regarding the non-submission of the BRS, the prolonged dormancy of the accounts and the absence of comprehensive records, particularly those prior to CY 2018, have rendered the preparation of a standard BRS unfeasible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the CAD to facilitate the submission of the aforecited DVs together with the necessary supporting documents to the COA for audit. Likewise, submit the required complete documentations to substantiate the recorded liquidations amounting to P24,000,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The CAD is currently in the process of tracing all supporting documents and will submit the necessary and complete documentation to substantiate the recorded liquidations.
observations.reason: The retrieval of supporting documents is currently ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require:

The CAD to determine the composition and details of the PPE items lumped and recorded as Beginning Balances, and without appropriate description amounting to P37,637,898.70 and P1,397,444.92, respectively;

The IC to establish the costs of the 41 units of PPE items following the guidelines and procedures prescribed in Item 6.2.12 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; 

The General Services Department (GSD) to verify whether missing PPE were already disposed or transferred/donated to other government agencies. If not, demand the immediate production of the subject PPE from the concerned accountable officers; and

The CAD and GSD to:

Verify/validate the PPE items included in the RPCPPE but not recognized in the books as well as those PPE taken up in the books but not in the RPCPPE in the amounts of P554,115,773.13 and P56,858,918.31, respectively, and effect the necessary adjustments to correct the same; and

Extend full support and assistance to the IC in the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to ensure the accuracy and fairness of its presentation in the Financial Statements (FS).
observations.managementAction:
The CAD has already exerted efforts to locate documents to trace the breakdown of the lump-sum balances; however, this has proven to be impossible.

All of the 41 items were included in the RCPPE during the previous administrations.  However, after identifying the market value, only two communication equipment were accounted for as PPE, while other items with a cost of P50,000.00 and below were considered as semi-expendable properties.

The City has already issued a demand letter to the accountable person to immediately produce the PPE, and failure to do so, the accountable person will pay for the missing items, subject to the rules and regulations of government accounting.

The lapsing schedule by the CAD and the RPCPPE by the GSD have been duly reconciled.
observations.reason:
Management acknowledges that they were unable to complete the entire One-Time Cleansing within the three-month period, as various operational challenges — particularly the delayed submission of reports necessary for derecognition entries — contributed to the delay.

No additional records or supporting documents have been identified to substantiate the lump-sum beginning balance.

There are still unrecorded balances in the SL of the CAD. The recommendations will be implemented in the conduct of the One-Time Cleansing.

The recommendations will be implemented in the conduct of the One-Time Cleansing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the OsMal to:

Immediately file M/R to PhilHealth for the denied claims to warrant the acceptance, processing and approval of the same, as well as those claims not submitted within the prescribed period; and

Strictly file the reimbursement claims within the 60 calendar days prescribed period pursuant to Section 46 of RIRR of RA No. 7875.
observations.managementAction:
The procurement of a new SERVER for Integrated Hospital Operations and Management Information System (iHOMIS) to upgrade the Hospital’s Information System is underway.

The completion of required documents for submission to PhilHealth is currently ongoing.
observations.reason:
Claims submitted to PhilHealth was returned as Return to Hospital because the attached documents were not processed/read by the PhilHealth system. According to PhilHealth, the Hospital’s Information System (LEXSYS) needs upgrading. 

On-going completion of documents to PhilHealth.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the LCCAP Core Team to regularly monitor and update the LCCAP to ensure that the PPAs indicated therein were climate-responsive.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE issued EO No. 24-02-14-005B creating the Technical Working Group (TWG) and planning core for the formulation of the Enhanced LCCAP.

The formulation of an enhanced LCCAP is currently 
on-going.
observations.reason: The formulation of an enhanced LCCAP is currently on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the SWMB to undertake regular review and updating of the SWMP to ensure that the policies and plan of actions are responsive to the recent developments for an effective management of solid waste.
observations.managementAction: The City in coordination with Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) conducted the WACs Study which is part of the updating process.
observations.reason: The 10-year SSWM Plan for Malabon City covering CYs 2025-2034 is on its final stage of writing to be discussed at the MCSWMB Meeting and later on to be submitted to the Metro Manila Development Authority (MMDA) for review. After its approval, regular review will be conducted by the MCSWMB, preferably a mid-year assessment and review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the MCSWMB thru the CENRO to provide full assistance and support to the concerned Barangays in looking for conducive locations for the setting up of MRF, and to ensure the functionality of all existing MRF facilities pursuant to Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
During the meetings conducted in 
CY 2024, the 21 barangays presented the status of their MRFs and other mechanism used as alternative to the implementation of MRF.
observations.reason: The barangays cited the lack of available space as a constraint for the establishment of MRFs.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Head of IASO make a representation to the Sangguniang Panlungsod to facilitate the amendment of City Ordinance No. AO4-2019 that will establish an IASO in conformity with the standards prescribed in IAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The City had drafted an ordinance concerning the creation of additional plantilla position essential for the proposed division.
observations.reason: The approval of the ordinance for the creation of the necessary plantilla positions is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation and  Management agreed to direct the CAD to initiate the preparation of necessary documents that will support the filing of the request to COA for authority to write-off of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The CAD had dispatched demand letters via registered mail to all identified parties.
observations.reason: The write-off was disapproved/postponed due to insufficient supporting documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the:

CAD to facilitate the remittances of employees’ contributions and loans payments to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth to avoid incurrence of penalty; and

HRMDD to require from the prospective employees the strict submission of complete data/information to include Pag-IBIG, GSIS, and PhilHealth Numbers.
observations.managementAction:
The updates and coordination with various offices are ongoing to ensure that all aspects are addressed in a timely manner.

According to HRMDD all outlined actions are in progress and partially updated. the procedures have been successfully integrated, with ongoing updates and coordination efforts ensuring that all
aspects are addressed in a timely manner.
observations.reason: The process of retrieving old employee data is intricate and time-consuming, contributing to the delays in completion.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the CGSD and CAD reconcile the discrepancy of P11.910 million between the inventory balance per books and RPCI in accordance with Section 491(i)(b) 
Volume I of the GAAM;
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation of the noted variance per books as against the inventory report not undertaken by the CAD and GSD.
observations.reason: There is a lack of appropriate action by the concerned GSD and CAD personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

Remit immediately the unremitted contributions and loan amortizations withheld from officers and employees to the GSIS in the amount of P5.036 million;

Remit the contributions and loan amortizations withheld within 10 days after the end of each month; and

Henceforth, ensure prompt and full remittance of contributions and loan amortizations withheld pursuant to Sections 14, 15 and 16 of the IRR of RA No. 8291.
observations.managementAction:
The CAD was able to remit the amount of P2.703 million during the year. 

In CY 2024, the CAD has remitted the contributions and loan amortizations withheld within 
10 days after the end of each month. 

The concerned offices will observe compliance with the relevant provisions of the IRR of RA 
No. 8291 regarding the withholding and remittance of contributions and loan payments.
observations.reason:
Lack of employee’s vital information necessary in the remittance of contributions and loan payments to the GSIS.

The concerned CAD personnel did not undertake the prompt and full remittance of contributions and loan amortizations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:
The CAD and CTD to:
Effect the necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts, upon receipt of the documents or proof of transactions from the banks concerned and/or as warranted by transaction documents;
Issue request with PNB for documents/information, copy furnished the head offices, for the possibility of securing related archived documents/information; and
Properly document and adjust in the books the derecognition of the bank accounts declared as closed by the PNB.
CAD to prepare the BRS for all bank accounts in compliance with 
Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-01.

We recommended that Management require the CAD to strictly enforce the liquidation of CAs granted to AOs within the reglementary period and to enforce the liquidation of CAs in compliance with Sections 5.1 and 5.8 of COA Circular 
No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
We recommended that Management refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers whose previous cash advances are not yet settled pursuant to Section 89 of PD 1445 and Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We recommended that Management to reclassify the amount of P10,636,347.03 recorded under the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account to the appropriate account Advances to Officers and Employees in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure proper financial reporting and compliance with accounting standards.

We recommended that Management require the:
OsMal to retrieve the records of PhilHealth claims submitted for the period November to December 2023, and upon retrieval, furnish a copy to the CAD for the proper recording and adjustment of the City's books of accounts;
OsMal to regularly provide the CAD with the list of submitted/in-process PhilHealth claims as basis in recording the receivables and the corresponding income in the books of accounts; and
CAD to immediately record in the books of accounts the receivables from PhilHealth amounting to P14,444,315.00 pertaining to services rendered by OsMal to member patients from January to December 2024.

We recommended that the Management require the CAD to:
Effect the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts; and
Set up an effective review level within the CAD so as to detect errors, inaccuracies and oversight in the accounting process to ensure accurate computation and recording of depreciation.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to:
Facilitate the remittance of taxes withheld from officers/employees, contractors/suppliers and other entities amounting to P1,760,454.67 in pursuant to Section 2.58(a) of BIR Revenue Regulations (RR) No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998; and
Direct the concerned CAD personnel to check and verify from the City’s accounting system the total or actual amount of taxes to be remitted to the BIR to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the same.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the CAD to facilitate the remittances of employees’ contributions and loan payments to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth pursuant to RA No. 8291 or the 
GSIS Act of 1997, RA No. 9679 or the HDMF Law of 2008 and National Health Insurance Act of 2013 (RA No. 7875 as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606) to avoid incurrence of penalty.

We recommended that the Management:
Ensure that fund realignments are conducted within reasonable time to allow sufficient lead time for the completion of procurement activities within the calendar year; and
Regularly monitor project implementation to ensure programmed activities are executed as planned.

We recommended that the Management:
Ensure the efficient and optimal utilization of the 20% DF for all PPAs by implementing the PPAs at the scheduled time frame pursuant to DILG-DOF-DBM JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020; and
Require the Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted, to present fairly the balance of the Accounts Payable at year-end.

We recommended that Management:
Require the LCCAP Core Team to regularly review and update the LCCAP to ensure that the City’s climate change adaptation and mitigation PPAs are appropriately prioritized based on the new information or data gathered and integrated into the City’s comprehensive plans; and
Include representatives from the Heath and Treasury Department and the LGOO in the LCCAP Core Team as required by the DILG MC.

We recommended that Management require the SWMB to conduct regular reviews and updates of the SWMP to ensure that solid waste management programs and projects remain aligned with recent developments in the field.

We recommended that Management direct the SWMB, through the CENRO, to exert more effort in identifying areas within the barangays where MRFs could be established, ensure the proper functioning of all existing facilities, and facilitate the establishment of a centralized MRF to serve multiple barangays pursuant to Section 32 of RA No. 9003.
We recommended that the Management to continue implementing the LDRRMF PPAs to strengthen the City’s disaster preparedness and response capabilities.

We recommended that Management continue its current practices in the implementation of the MCCPC’s PPAs and remain focused on achieving the City’s goal of effectively mainstreaming the rights and welfare of children in its development plans. By sustaining these efforts, the MCCPC can further enhance its impact on child protection and contribute to the continued well-being and development of children in Malabon City.

We recommended that the Management direct the Gender & Development Focal Point System (GFPS) to:
Closely coordinate with the implementing offices to monitor the status of GAD PPAs to guarantee full execution of the proposed projects in accordance with the DILG-endorsed GPB and ensure that GAD funds are properly allocated to programs that fulfill the core objective of addressing the City’s gender mandates and issues; and
Require the GAD Office to ensure timely submission of the GPB and GAD AR to the OCA to facilitate evaluation and validation of all reported GAD activities and accomplishments.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:

GSD to prepare and submit SSMI to the CAD for recording in the books of accounts the cost of inventories issued during the year, for the fair presentation of the Inventory Accounts in the FS;

CAD and GSD to maintain SLCs and SCs, respectively, for each type of supplies and inventory items in compliance with Sections 114, 119 and 120, Chapter 6, of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and

CAD to disclose in the Notes to FS the amount of inventories recognized as expense during the audit period pursuant to Paragraphs 47(d) and 49 of IPSAS 12 – Inventories.
observations.managementAction:
The GSD had prepared and submitted to CAD in CY 2024.

The CAD will commence the preparation of the SLC for supplies inventory items in CY 2025.

Inventories recognized as expense in 
CY 2024 is disclosed in the Notes to FS.
observations.reason: The cause of delay is due to the lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require:

The CAD to:

Strictly observe the provisions of Section 4(o) of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, in the computation and imposition of depreciation on all City’s PPE;

Prepare journal entry voucher to correct and adjust/recognize the noted deficiencies in the computations of depreciations amounting to P33,292,953.06 and P212,917.89. Likewise, effect the necessary corrections on the noted errors in the preparation of the PPE lapsing schedule to present the accurate amount of depreciation; and

Record the procured PPE at delivery date pursuant to Section 120 of NGAS Manual, Volume I, to ensure that the appropriate deprecation will be computed and imposed on the said properties.

The GSD to regularly submit copy of Acceptance & Inspection Reports (AIRs) pertaining to PPE items procured to the CAD to serve as basis of the latter in recording the same in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

GSD to monitor, account and record all inventories and PPE acquired through donations, and ensure to provide the CAD with copy of pertinent documents as regards the donated items to serve as basis in recording the same in the books of accounts pursuant to Item V-b and h of the City Memorandum No. 21, s. of 2023; 

CAD to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to record in the books the value of the Inventories and PPE items received by the City through donations; and 

GSD and CAD to determine the value of the seven PPE items without cost. If efforts proved futile, require the appraisal committee to appraise the value of the subject PPE.
observations.managementAction:
The GSD had transmitted all supporting documents of PPEs acquired through donations to CAD.

The CAD has already made the required journal entries for adjustment via JEV No. 2024-04-003 and 2024-04-004 regarding PPE items received by the city through donations.

The cost of the two PPE items has already been determined.
observations.reason: The process of locating the Property Transfer Receipts (PTR) and other pertinent documents is currently ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the: 

CAD to make necessary adjustments in the books for the returned goods and services for P15,065,140.00; and

GSD and Inspection Committee to revisit and implement the required inspection procedures as provided under Sec. 118 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
The returned goods were already delivered by the supplier and paid in CY 2024.  

The GSD had issued a Memorandum regarding the simplification of the procurement process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the CAD to:

Stop the practice of remitting in advance the employees’ contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS and Pag-IBIG, and to regularly review the amount of taxes to be remitted to the BIR, to avoid the occurrence of overpayment/over remittance; and

Conduct thorough review and evaluation on the noted abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments to correct the same.
observations.managementAction:
The Remittance Section under the CAD was instructed to refrain from remitting in advance the employee's contributions and loan amortizations to Pag-IBIG and GSIS through the issuance of a Memorandum.

In CY 2024, the noted abnormal balances were already corrected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the:

Property and the Accounting Offices to conduct again the preliminary activities following strictly the procedures enumerated in Item 6.1 of COA Circular No. 2020-006, to arrive at an accurate and reliable inventory working papers.

IC to facilitate the completion of the cleansing activities by consistently observing the general and procedural guidelines prescribed in the same Circular for the fair presentation of the PPE account balances in the SFP.
observations.managementAction:
The CAD conducted the preliminary activities needed in the inventory working papers for their initiative in the conduct of the 
one-time cleansing in CY 2024.

The GSD will coordinate with all the concerned departments and offices to facilitate the initial steps of the one-time cleansing procedure for the City’s PPE.
observations.reason: The PPE reconciliation between the CAD and GSD is currently ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the LSB to:

Facilitate the full implementation of all planned PPAs by addressing the challenges encountered in the preparation and completion of needed documents related to the procurement of PPAs to avoid delays and/or non- implementation of the same; and

Formulate PPAs for NFP as required in Sec. 1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2020, to ensure that the needs of the undernourished learners in public schools will be addressed.
observations.managementAction:
The City had implemented a monthly monitoring of physical and financial accomplishment and evaluation of PPAs based on the approved Work and Financial plan, to ensure timely implementation of all PPAs.  

Discussed with the Local School Board the list of PPAs to address the undernourished learners which were submitted to the City Nutrition Office for incorporation in the City Nutrition Action Plan.

observations.recommendation: To attain the optimal utilization of the 20% DF, we recommended that the City Mayor require the MCDC to properly identify the specific priority development PPAs to be funded by the DF, and to efficiently implement these PPAs to ensure its immediate completion for the benefits of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, the City Planning Department coordinated with the City Engineering Department, as project implementer on the status of the PPAs under the 20% DF for CY 2024. They had also closely monitored the utilization and implementation of the 20% DF PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the CDRRMC to regularly review and update the LDRRMFIP to ensure that all unexpended balance of DRRMF of previous years transferred to the STF are allocated to identified DRRM PPAs, to attain the optimum utilization of the disaster fund.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2024, the City implemented 71.43 percent of the PPAs from the unexpended balance of DRRMF of previous years transferred to the STF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor designate additional eligible personnel to help and assist the MCCPC in its efficient operation. Likewise, require the Council to monitor the full implementation and completion of all planned PPAs to ensure the attainment of its objectives.
observations.managementAction: In coordination with the City Budget Department, the CPDD closely monitored the utilization of the 1% LCPC PPA through the Monthly Accomplishment of Monitoring of Physical Accomplishment and Physical and Financial Evaluation Form.

observations.recommendation:
To improve the City’s lease contract administration, we recommended that the City Mayor direct the GSD to:

Maintain and keep complete records and information on leased properties necessary to effectively managed the same; 

Facilitate the timely renewal of the contract of lease in order to protect the interest of the City; and

Require the immediate payment of additional security deposit from the concerned tenants pursuant to the terms and conditions of the lease agreement.

Require the CAD to maintain SL for each of the lessee and indicate therein all the relevant information regarding the leased properties for an efficient and effective lease tracking, monitoring and accounting.
observations.managementAction:
The GSD now maintains complete records and information on the leased properties.

Upon establishing their records and information on leased properties, the GSD is now properly monitoring the timely renewal of leases.

Management has sent demand letters for the payment of additional security deposits, some lessees have already responded and made the corresponding payments.

The CAD now maintains SL for each lessees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head of PSTMO to require the designated ETOs of the PSTMO to intensify the collections of unpaid daily fees/contributions from the delinquent E-Trike recipients to have enough fund necessary to support the operation, maintenance and upkeep of the E-Trikes as well as to expand the e-vehicle initiatives in the City for the provision of public transport.
observations.managementAction:
The management has intensified their collection efforts. Comparing the 2nd semester collection of 
CY 2023 amounting to P554,625.00, with the 1st semester collection of 
CY 2024 amounting to P1,138,500.00, with an increase of P583,875.00.

b. Strictly file the reimbursement claims within the 60 calendar days prescribed period pursuant to Section 46 of RIRR of RA No. 7875.

We recommended the LSB to:
a. Facilitate the full implementation of all planned PPAs by addressing the challenges encountered in the preparation and completion of needed documents related to the procurement of PPAs to avoid delays and/or non- implementation of the same; and
c. Formulate PPAs for NFP as required in Sec. 1 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2020, to ensure that the needs of the undernourished learners in public schools will be addressed.

To attain the optimal utilization of the 20% DF, we recommended that the City Mayor require the MCDC to properly identify the specific priority development PPAs to be funded by the DF, and to efficiently implement these PPAs to ensure its immediate completion for the benefits of the intended beneficiaries.
We recommended that the City Mayor require the LCCAP Core Team to regularly monitor and update the LCCAP to ensure that the PPAs indicated therein were climate-responsive.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the SWMB to undertake regular review and updating of the SWMP to ensure that the policies and plan of actions are responsive to the recent developments for an effective management of solid waste.

We recommended the MCSWMB thru the CENRO to provide full assistance and support to the concerned Barangays in looking for conducive locations for the setting up of MRF, and to ensure the functionality of all existing MRF facilities pursuant to Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CDRRMC to regularly review and update the LDRRMFIP to ensure that all unexpended balance of DRRMF of previous years transferred to the STF are allocated to identified DRRM PPAs, to attain the optimum utilization of the disaster fund.

We recommended that the City Mayor designate additional eligible personnel to help and assist the MCCPC in its efficient operation. Likewise, require the Council to monitor the full implementation and completion of all planned PPAs to ensure the attainment of its objectives.

To improve the City’s lease contract administration, we recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct the CGSD to:
Maintain and keep complete records and information on leased properties necessary to effectively managed the same;
Facilitate the timely renewal of the contract of lease in order to protect the interest of the City; and
Require the immediate payment of additional security deposit from the concerned tenants pursuant to the terms and conditions of the lease agreement.
Require the CAD to maintain SL for each of the lessee and indicate therein all the relevant information regarding the leased properties for an efficient and effective lease tracking, monitoring and accounting.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head of PSTMO to require the designated ETOs of the PSTMO to intensify the collections of unpaid daily fees/contributions from the delinquent E-Trike recipients to have enough fund necessary to support the operation, maintenance and upkeep of the E-Trikes as well as to expand the e-vehicle initiatives in the City for the provision of public transport.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Head of IASO make a representation to the Sangguniang Panlungsod to facilitate the amendment of City Ordinance No. AO4-2019 that will establish an IASO in conformity with the standards prescribed in IAM for LGUs.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation and  Management agreed to direct the CAD to initiate the preparation of necessary documents that will support the filing of the request to COA for authority to write-off of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CAD to:
Facilitate the remittance in full of all taxes withheld to the BIR pursuant to Section 2.58(a) of BIR Revenue Regulations (RR) No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998;
Direct the concerned CAD personnel to check and verify from the accounting system the total or actual amount of taxes to be remitted to the BIR to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the remittance to be made; and
Strengthen internal control by meticulously observing the review process in the approval of amount of taxes to be remitted to the BIR to avoid incurrence of under/over remittance of taxes withheld.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
CAD to facilitate the remittances of employees’ contributions and loans payments to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth to avoid incurrence of penalty; and
HRMDD to require from the prospective employees the strict submission of complete data/information to include Pag-IBIG, GSIS, and PhilHealth Numbers.

Inform the respective accountable officers to return the unserviceable PPEs to GSD for cancellation of Acknowledgment Receipt of Equipment for proper accountability;

No. 92-386; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 167 of COA Circular 
No. 92-386 for subsequent disposal of unserviceable PPEs.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit the RPCIs for CY 2021 immediately to facilitate the timely review of financial transactions and ascertain the correctness of the amount recorded in the Inventories account of the City; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that:
OsMal submit to CAD and CTD the necessary documents relative to claims from PhilHealth and its corresponding payments to facilitate the recording of Due from GOCCs and accrued revenue accounts;
The CAD to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize Due from GOCCs and related accrued revenue accounts; and
Henceforth, strictly observe Paragraph 19 of IPSAS 9 – Revenue from Exchange Transactions and Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, for the accounting and recognition of Due from GOCCs, respectively.

We recommended that the Management required the CAD to:
Prepare the necessary documentation to support the request for write-off in accordance with the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005;
Afterwards, submit to COA the request for authority to write-off its Due from Officers and Employees and Other Receivables balances that are already determined to be dormant; and
Henceforth, strictly observe compliance with the guidelines and procedures on write-off of dormant receivables prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that Management require the CSWMB, as part of their duties and responsibilities, to monitor the established MRFs in various barangays to ensure that it operates as to its purpose and functionality that will contribute to the attainment of the City’s Solid Waste Management Plan pursuant to Section 1 and Section 1 (d) of Rule XI of IRR of RA 
No. 9003.

We recommended that the Management coordinate with the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the enactment of an Ordinance that will facilitate:
The creation of a Management Audit Division and Operations Audit Division for the IASO in order to achieve the appropriate organizational structure prescribed by Part 2 of IAM for LGUs;
The duties and responsibilities of the IASO personnel are ensured to conform with the roles and responsibilities provided in Part 2 of IAM for LGUs in order to carry out the services expected of an IAS/U; and
Strict compliance with the provisions of Part 2 of the IAM for LGUs, moving forward.

We recommended that the Management require the CAD to:
Remit immediately the taxes withheld from officers/employees, contractors/ suppliers and other entities amounting to P1,024,383.99 to the BIR;
Regularly remit the withheld taxes within ten (10) days after the end of each month except for taxes withheld for December which shall be filed on or before January 25 of the following year; and
Henceforth, ensure prompt and full remittance of taxes withheld pursuant to Section 2.58 of BIR RR No. 2-98 dated May 17, 1998, as amended to avoid incurrence of interest as defined under Section 11 of BIR RR No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018.

We recommended that Management:
The CTD to remit immediately the unremitted contributions and loan amortizations withheld from officers and employees to the Pag-IBIG in the amount of P991,274.19;
The CHRMDD to coordinate and gather from the employees the necessary information and Pag-IBIG Members’ Identification Number in order to remit the prior year’s unremitted collections; and
Prompt and full remittance of contributions and loan amortizations withheld pursuant to Section 3, Rule VII of the IRR of RA No. 9679, moving forward.

We recommended that the Management:
Immediately remit the Agency’s and employees’ share amounting to P169,203.65 to the PhilHealth; and
Strictly observe the timely remittance of monthly premiums contributions every 11th-15th day of the month following the applicable period pursuant to Section 18(b) and (f), Rule III, Title III of the Revised IRR of the National Health Insurance Act of 2013, and PhilHealth Circular No. 0001 s. 2014, dated January 9, 2014.
We recommended that the Management immediately enforce the settlement of all audit disallowances in compliance with Sections 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that:
The CGSD conduct an inventory of supplies and materials as of 
year-end and submit the RPCI to the CAD for reconciliation and adjustment, if any, and to the Auditor’s Office in accordance with Section 124 Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs;
The CAD prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock to ensure that all purchases and issuances of supplies and materials are recorded, in conformity with Section 120 Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
The CAD reconcile accounting records with the CGSD records and RPCI to arrive at the correct amount of inventories as of year-end.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that:
The City General Services Department (CGSD), CAD and the Inventory Committee complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting and property records.
The CAD effect the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the PPE accounts based on the results of the reconciliations made.
The CGSD to –
Cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable items and the CAD to record the dropping from the books of the items disposed; and
Initiate the process of transferring the respective TCT’s in the City’s name to establish its rightful ownership.
We recommended that Management cause the reversion to the GF the Other Payable accounts of P3,369,456.29 which remained outstanding and unclaimed for more than two years to 11 years.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management direct the CAD to maintain subsidiary ledger records and update the postings of transactions thereat, record the transactions for the redeemed delinquent real property amounting to P683,457.46 and adjust the understatement of P2,181.71 to reflect the correct balances of the said account.
We also recommended that the CAD analyze the  recording of the refund of redemption price in 2014 to determine to what accounts  these were misposted.
We recommended that Management return the unutilized balances of fund transfers in the total amount of P9,037,751.75 to the respective Source Agencies.

We recommended that the balances of Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees accounts, which have remained unliquidated for two years or more, be reverted to the Other Income account of the City’s General Fund.

We recommended that Management:
Evaluate the cost/benefit between obtaining credit financing and in maintaining time deposits, in line with Section 2 of PD No. 1445; and
Pay the loan in full to avoid incurrence of additional interest/loss in the amount of P28,999,465.75.

We recommended that the City officials concerned undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the basis of reasonable and/or realistic projection of revenues for the ensuing year, in accordance with the intent of local government budgeting that will support the proper allocation of expenditures as provided under Section 316 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the City Accountant examine the validity and actual existence of –
The Other Receivable accounts which remained dormant for more than two to 36 years and make the necessary adjustment/s in the books, if warranted; and
The overpayment to Officers and Employees which remained dormant for more than three to 10 years, and make the necessary adjustment/s in the books, if warranted.
We recommended that the Management cause the recording of the donated E-trikes amounting to P93,314,326.53 in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Maintain SLs for each e-trike showing the contribution of the recipients  to the ECTA fund for proper monitoring;
Amend the agreement or “Kasunduan” by providing specific number of days per week on the use of e-trikes to achieve the five-year earning period as provided by DOE; and
Regularly monitor the payments of the ECTA fund to prevent under-collection of remittances and to encourage e-trike recipients to remit weekly.
We recommended that Management effect immediately the adjustments on the deposits made to the ECTA Fund and the corresponding cash accounts.

We recommended that:
The CTD, particularly the disbursing officer, submit disbursement vouchers and payrolls with complete supporting documents to the CAD within the prescribed period; and
The CAD install measures that will facilitate the timely submission of disbursement vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, accounting reports and financial statements to the City Auditor’s Office within the required first ten days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System cause the development of strategies and plans for tools and processes to be used by the Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) Team in the evaluation of outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System conduct gender analysis and give priority in gathering sufficient sex-disaggregated data particularly those data or information from barangays that should form part of City’s GAD database.

We recommended that the City’s Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (CDRRMO) monitor the implementation of programmed projects and ensure completion thereof within the timeline in order that Disaster Preparedness and Disaster Response may be efficiently and effectively served.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the City consider prioritization of the establishment of the required Materials Recovery Facility in every barangay or cluster of barangays in the City of Malabon in compliance with Section 32 of Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommended that management fill up the newly created positions with qualified and competent personnel and reiterate the prior year’s audit recommendation that Management create and organize an IAS independent of the CAD in order to strengthen the internal control system.

We recommended that:
The City Treasurer remit immediately to the BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth the unremitted taxes, contributions and loan payments;
Management make representations with concerned agencies for the application of the overpayment to the subsequent remittances of the City; and
The CAD institute appropriate measures to minimize if not eliminate the occurrence of over/double remittances or overpayment of salaries.

observations.recommendation:
1. We reiterated last year’s recommendations that:


       a. The CGSD and the Inventory Committee complete the reconciliation
          of the RPCPPE with the CAD and CGSD records;


       b. The CAD –


           • Effect the necessary adjustments to correct  the  balances  of
             the PPE accounts based on the  results  of  the  reconciliation
             made;


           • Reclassify items costing below P10,000.00 to the appropriate
             semi-expendable accounts; and

       c. The CGSD cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable items and
          the CAD to record  the  dropping  from  the  books  of  the  items
          disposed.

1. We reiterated the prior year’s recommendations that -


        a. The City  Treasurer  coordinate  with  the  depository  banks  as
           regards the documents to support the unadjusted and  unidentified
           reconciling items under the GF;


        b. The City Accountant -


           • Assign personnel to prepare the BRS on a monthly basis for  all
             depository accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011; and


           • Effect immediately the  adjusting  entries  in  the  books  for
             reconciling items identified in the BRS.

1. We recommended that Management require:


         a. The Inventory Committee to  conduct  a  semestral  inventory  of
            supplies and materials and prepare inventory report which  shall
            be reconciled with the CGSD and CAD records before submission to
            the City Auditor’s Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume
            I of the NGAS Manual; and


         b. The CAD to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards  to  ensure  that  all
            purchases and issuances of supplies and materials are  recorded,
            in conformity with Section 120, Volume I of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended the following:

On revenue accounts that reported decreases in collections -


        a. The City  Business  Permits  and  Licensing  Department  (CBPLD)
           conduct  an  analysis  of  the   identified   revenue   accounts
           pertaining to business tax to address the  decreasing  trend  in
           collection; and


        b. The City Treasury Department (CTD) look into the possible causes
           of the decline in the identified revenue accounts pertaining  to
           RPT and strategize to improve collection; and

        On  revenue  accounts  that  reported  collections  without   income
        estimates and those revenue accounts that have targets  but  without
        reported collections –


        c. The Committee faithfully exercise its mandate under Section 316,
           Article I, Chapter  III  of  the  said  Act  to  have  realistic
           projections for each  revenue  accounts  and  exert  efforts  to
           collect the projected amount.

1. In view thereof, we  recommended  that  the  Management  cause  the
   reversion to the GF of Accounts Payable and  Due  to  Officers  and
   Employees accounts which remained outstanding and unclaimed for two
   years or more.

1. We recommended that Management:

        a. Incorporate in the contract agreement the salient features under
           Sections 6.4-10, 7.1a-e and 7.2a-d and 11 of City Ordinance  No.
           09-2003, dated July 17, 2003 to ensure  compliance  and  enforce
           sanctions for non-compliance therewith; and


        b. Provide procedures for  the  computation  of  deduction  against
           monthly billing whenever the  garbage  collected  is  below  the
           minimum requirement of 700 cubic meter per day.

1. We recommended that the Management consider prioritization  of  the
   establishment of the required MRF in every barangay or a cluster of
   barangays in the City in compliance with Section 32 of RA No. 9003.

1. We recommended that Management  strengthen  and  develop  the  GAD
   Database system to include  sex  disaggregated  data  and  provide
   technical assistance to the City’s  Planning  Office  in  ensuring
   that the established GAD Database is systematically operating.

1. We recommended that Management  ensure  that  the  GFPS  Executive
   Committee    and    the    TWG    representatives     from     the
   committees/councils,   national/regional   government    agencies,
   academe, private sector and  civil  society  organizations  (CSOs)
   operating at the local level for effective  gender  mainstreaming,
   and have establish a permanent GAD Office.

1. We recommended that the City’s LDRRMO:

          a. Submit to the CAO the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization
             of DRRMF  on or before the 15th day after the end of each month
             as required under Section 5.1.5 of COA  Circular  No.  2012-002
             dated September 12, 2012; and


          b. Furnish the CAO with a copy of the Annual  Work  and  Financial
             Plan  as  basis  in  the  evaluation  and  monitoring  of   the
             implementation of LDRRMs PPAs.

1. We recommended that the City Mayor:

        a. Direct the City official concerned to comply with the  pertinent
           provisions of JC No. 1, S-2017 by ensuring that  only  allowable
           expenditures shall be paid out of the SEF;


        b. Instruct the LSB that in the preparation of  the  Annual  School
           Board Budget, prioritize the PPAs pursuant to R.A. 5447, Section
           272 of R.A. 7160 and JC No. 01, S-2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG.

        c. Direct the LSB to stop appropriating and paying honoraria of the
           ALS trainers, fuel, oil and lubricants, repairs and maintenance,
           insurance premium and yearly renewal of  registration  of  LSB’s
           service vehicles as priority expenses  chargeable  against  SEF;
           and

        d. The Management cause the refund from the LDRRMF, the  amount  of
           P795,868.00 representing  the  cost  of  disaster  response  and
           rescue equipment charged to SEF.

1. We recommended that the City Mayor hire or  designate  a  new  Head
   either of the City Assessor’s Office or the  Accounting  Office  to
   ensure  that  no  one  person  should  be   responsible   for   two
   incompatible functions.

1. We recommended that:

        a. Management –


            • Exert efforts to recover the amount of lost/wasted funds  due
              to over/double remittances to different NGAs and  overpayment
              of employees’ salaries;


            • Identify the person/s who caused the  over/double  remittance
              or  overpayment and make him/her/them accountable; and


        b. The CAD institute  appropriate  measures  to  minimize,  if  not
           eliminate, the occurrence of over/double remittances.

1. We recommended that:

        a) The City Treasurer remit immediately to the BIR, GSIS,  Pag-IBIG
           and PhilHealth the  unremitted  taxes,  contributions  and  loan
           payments;


        b)  Management  exert  efforts  to  recover  the  amount   due   to
           over/double remittances to the different NGAs, and identify  the
           person/s who caused the over/double  remittance  or  overpayment
           and make him/her/them accountable; and


        c) The CAD institute appropriate measures to minimize,  if  not  to
           eliminate, the occurrence of over/double remittances.

1. We reiterated the previous year’s recommendations that  the  Local
         Chief Executive direct concerned officials to coordinate with  all
         persons responsible/liable and facilitate the  settlement  of  all
         outstanding audit suspensions and disallowances as required  under
         the existing rules and  regulations.  It  was  also  advised  that
         unsettled disallowances be endorsed to the City Legal  Office  for
         necessary action/disposition.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that:
The City Treasurer coordinate with the depository banks as regards to the documents to support the unadjusted and unidentified reconciling items under the General Fund;
The City Mayor through the Legal Office demand justification/information from the City Treasurer on the unjustified delay in the submission of disbursement vouchers which are not supported with documents; and
The City Accountant:
Assign personnel to prepare the BRS on a monthly basis for all depository accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011; and
Effect adjusting entries immediately in the books of accounts for reconciling items already identified.

We reiterated our last year’s recommendations that:
The Inventory Committee and CAD complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting records; and
The CAD effect the adjustments, if any, to correct the overstatement/ understatement of PPE as a result of the reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting and property records.

We recommended that:
The CAD effect the adjusting entry/ies to correct the Other Payables and Deferred Credits and Income accounts;
The City Treasury Department -
Transfer the fund from the Trust Fund account to the General Fund account; and
Enforce collection of the unpaid balance of the total assessed amount of RPT.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the CAD to identify the causes of the difference on the recorded unutilized amount in the books vis-à-vis the confirmation from DPWH and effect the necessary adjustments to book unrecorded liquidation and/or unrecorded utilization of financial assistance-PBM, if any; and
Direct the City Treasurer to return the unutilized balance to the DPWH and DOH.

We recommended that the CAD make appropriate adjusting entries for the transfer of the CIP - Building and Other Structures account to the Building and Other Structures account in view of the completion of the PBM project.

We recommended that the City Accounting Department deduct from the salaries of the concerned personnel the amounts of over/double payments. Henceforth, institute appropriate measures to minimize if not eliminate the occurrence of over/double remittances or overpayment of salaries.

We recommended that:
The City Accounting Department -
Effect adjusting entry/ies to correct the balances of the Due from NGAs-BIR and Due from Officers and Employees to present fairly the account balances in the books; and
Institute internal control measures to prevent recurrence of double remittance of withholding taxes; and
The City Treasurer exert efforts for the immediate recovery of the over remittance to BIR.

We recommended that the City Treasurer and the City Accountant strictly enforce compliance by all accountable officers for the liquidation of their Petty Cash accountabilities and return to the City Treasurer all unused cash at the end of the year pursuant to Section 48, Volume 1 of the NGAs Manual.

We recommended that:
The CAD record under the Due to NGAs account the difference between the computed mandatory MMDA shares vis-a-vis the amount deducted by DBM for said MMDA shares for CYs 2011 to 2017 in the net amount of P1,386,814.74; and
Management ensure the accrual of 5% of the City’s total gross revenue of the preceding year, net of the internal revenue allotment, as the City’s share in the operations of the MMDA, and the timely remittance of the amount to the said agency pursuant to Section 10(d) of R.A. No. 7924.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the City Accounting Department to -
Prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the Cost of Sales and RPT dues, and transactions pertaining to the redeemed delinquent real property; and
Maintain SL records for Other Payables account (TF) and update the postings in the SLs in accordance with Section 10, Volume II of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
Exert efforts to recover the over remittances of loan payments to MCGEPC and LBP.
We recommended that Management create and organize an internal audit service independent of the Accounting Department.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the City Accountant record the income earned from the conduct of public bidding as trust receipts in accordance with the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A and transfer the excess amount to the General Fund pursuant to the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A.

We recommended that the CAD ensure that claims for honoraria of BAC members, TWG and Secretariat are supported with the required documents as provided under item 5.7.1 of COA 2012-001 including Office Order and Notice of Awards to establish validity and propriety of the claims.

We recommended that:
The City Assessor -
Maintain and keep an updated record of all idle lands located within the City of Malabon; and
Furnish the Office of the City Treasurer with an updated record of idle lands for imposition of additional tax in accordance with Section 239 of RA 7160 and Section 2B.06, Article B of City Ordinance No. A10-2016.
The City Treasurer -
Send notice of assessment to all owners of idle lands in the City; and
Impose and collect ad valorem tax on all idle lands in the City.

We reiterated last year’s recommendation that Management direct:
The GSD and the City Accountant to insure that all government properties with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS against any loss or damage caused by fire, earthquake or other calamity; and
The Budget Officer to ensure that appropriate amount is included in the budget to cover for the property insurance premiums.

We reiterated our last years’ recommendation that:
The City Treasurer’s Office, particularly the disbursing officer, submit disbursement vouchers and payrolls with complete supporting documents; and
The CAD install measure that will facilitate the timely submission of disbursement vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, accounting reports and financial statements within the required first ten days of the ensuing month.
We recommended that the City Treasurer continue the practice of regularly paying the loan amortization.

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct concerned officials to coordinate with all persons responsible/liable and facilitate the settlement of all outstanding audit suspensions and disallowances as required under existing regulations. It was also advised that disallowances be endorsed to the City Legal Office for necessary action/disposition.

observations.managementAction: The City Engineering Department (CED) as one of the members of Malabon Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council is also in-charge of the stockpiling of construction materials for disaster preparedness. CED occupies a one storey type house previously used as a court office located at Barangay Catmon as a temporary warehouse. The magnitude of the fire incident at Dulong Hernadez St., Barangay Catmon traversing Barangay Tonsuya resulted in the bulk procurement of construction materials intended for disaster response and recovery and rehabilitation. It was stockpiled in one room beside the City Library. However, other materials such as galvanized iron sheets for roofing and good lumber were placed outside the warehouse because of its size and consideration that it can withstand adverse weather conditions. Nevertheless, they have implemented security measures pending the distribution of the materials to the recipients. There exist a CCTV and the warehouse is guarded and its compound where the materials are kept and guarded by our City Security Unit 24/7.
observations.reason:
Bidding was carried out end of December 2017 and the NTP was only issued on January 2018.

For inclusion in the DRRM plan CY 2018. Preparation of manual or brochures is in process.

A contractual employee was assigned as warehouse keeper, however, a permanent employee should be designated.

Accessibility to storage facilities are not restricted, withdrawals of goods are left to the discretion of the requesting end-user and checked or validated by the warehouse keeper afterwards. The goods and equipment are stored in 3 different storage facilities. No individual stock cards are maintained on each item. However, the warehouse keeper maintained monthly summary reports.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the prior year’s recommendations that -

the City Treasurer coordinate with the depository banks as regards to the documents to support the unadjusted and unidentified reconciling items under General Fund;
the Legal Office demand explanation/information from the City Treasurer on the unjustified delay in the verification of disbursements and conduct investigation for unsupported disbursements; and
the City Accountant, for SEF and TF, to
prepare the bank reconciliation statements on a monthly basis for all depository accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011; and
effect adjusting entries immediately in the books of accounts for reconciling items already identified and for the understatement of book balance under LBP Account No. 0652-1019-92

We reiterated the last year’s recommendations that –

the Inventory Committee complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the accounting and property records;

the City Accounting Department:

effect the adjustments to correct the overstatement of Land Improvements account and the corresponding understatement of affected PPE accounts; and

drop from the book of accounts the cost of demolished market structures duly supported with complete documentation.
We recommended that the CAD provide and compute the correct depreciation of PPEs and make the necessary   adjustment to the affected account.

We recommended that the City Accounting Department stop the practice of recording the costs of Fidelity Bond Premiums, Taxes, Duties and Licenses and Terminal Leave Benefits as part of the Insurance Expense account.

We recommended that –

the  City Accountant strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DECS/DBM/DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 1998 and refrain/stop from using the SEF for other purposes; and
the assigned DECS’s supply officer evaluate the inventory balance of medicine, medical, dental and laboratory supplies for turnover to the City Health Department.

We recommended that CAD effect the appropriate adjusting entry/s for the fund transfer, and submit the liquidation report together with the supporting documents to the Audit Team for audit verification.
We recommended that Management return the unutilized cash balance to the Source Agency/s if the same is no longer needed.

We recommended that the City Treasurer remit the full amount of P11,389,004.50 to the Bureau of Treasury which represents 20 per cent of the building permit fees collections covered by Department Order No. 51-2010.

We recommended that CSWDD -
submit the required supporting documents per transaction to ascertain propriety of disbursements for the Program; and
explain the double enrollment or listing of beneficiaries and correct the entries in the lists of beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) prepare the APP   in the prescribed format required under GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 to ensure the proper and efficient conduct of procurement activities in the implementation of programmed activities/projects and planned targets of the City.

We recommended that –
Management appoint a regular City Accountant, fill up the necessary vacant plantilla items in the CAD, re-evaluate the sufficiency of existing manpower complement of the Office and create additional position, if warranted, to avoid the delayed preparation and submission of financial reports and documents and the frequent rendition of overtime by the staff.
The City Treasurer’s Office, particularly the disbursing officer, submit disbursement vouchers and payrolls with complete supporting documents.
The CAD install measures that will facilitate timely recording of the transactions to ensure the timely submission of disbursement vouchers, payrolls, official receipts, accounting reports and financial statements within the required first 10 days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that Management direct –
the CGSO and the City Accountant to ensure that all government properties are insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS against any loss or damage caused by fire, earthquake or other calamity; and
the Budget Officer to ensure that appropriate amount is included in the budget to cover for the property insurance premiums.
We recommended that the City Accountant and LDRRMO review the submitted utilization report and make the necessary corrections or adjustments.

We recommended that the City Accountant –
strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the  accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF;
make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the entries made for the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, particularly on the account used and the total amount considered, as provided under COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF.
We recommended that the City Treasurer remit the amount of P24,265.65 to GSIS.
We have recommended that the City Treasurer remit the amount of P4,900.00 to Pag-IBIG.
We have recommended that the City Treasurer remit the amount of P5,002,057.64 to the BIR immediately.

We recommended that the City General Services Department and all Departments concerned comply with full disclosure policy as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-140 in order to inform the constituents of Malabon City and the general public of its accomplishments and how public funds were utilized.

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief   Executive-

direct concerned officials to coordinate with all persons responsible/liable and facilitate the settlement of all outstanding audit disallowances as required under existing regulations. It was also advised that disallowances be endorsed to the City Legal Office for necessary action/disposition;
remind concerned City officials and employees to immediately settle the disallowances which become final and executory; and
follow-up/verify the status of the compliance on the audit suspensions submitted, thru mail, to the Intelligence/ Confidential Fund Audit Unit, Office of the Chairperson, Commission on Audit.
